name: AD SOC Detection Lab — Attack Coverage
description: Detection coverage from Active Directory SOC Detection Lab — 7 attack scenarios simulated and detected using Elastic SIEM. Author: Atharva | MSc Cyber Security Engineering | University of Warwick

techniques.comment: IR-001: Password Spraying detected via Event ID 4625 + SubStatus 0xC000006A. KQL detection rule deployed in Elastic SIEM.
IR-001: Password Spraying — CrackMapExec via SMB. 12 failed logons detected. KQL: event.code:4625 + LogonType:3 + SubStatus:0xc000006a
IR-002: Kerberoasting detected via Event ID 4769 + TicketEncryptionType 0x17 (RC4-HMAC). KQL detection rule deployed.
IR-002: Kerberoasting — Impacket GetUserSPNs. RC4 TGS hash obtained for sqlsvc. KQL: event.code:4769 + TicketEncryptionType:0x17
IR-003 + IR-004: DCSync and LSASS Dump both detected. Full domain credential dump achieved.
IR-003: DCSync — Impacket secretsdump. 36 replication operations in 327ms. KQL: event.code:4662 + AccessMaskDescription:Control Access + ObjectServer:DS
IR-004: LSASS Dump — comsvcs.dll MiniDump via rundll32. GrantedAccess 0x1FFFFF. KQL: event.code:10 + TargetImage:lsass.exe + GrantedAccess:0x1fffff
IR-005: PsExec Lateral Movement detected via Event ID 7045. Random service name pattern identified.
IR-005: PsExec via SMB — Impacket. 5 random service installs detected on WIN10-Victim. KQL: event.code:7045 + exclusion of known-good services
IR-006: Pass-the-Hash detected via Event ID 4624 + NTLM auth from non-Windows source.
IR-006: Pass-the-Hash — Evil-WinRM. Administrator hash from DCSync used directly. KQL: event.code:4624 + LogonType:3 + AuthPackage:NTLM + source.ip:10.0.0.4
IR-007: Scheduled Task Persistence detected via Event ID 4698.
IR-007: Scheduled Task — schtasks. \WindowsUpdate task masquerading as legitimate process. KQL: event.code:4698 + not TaskName:\Microsoft\*
Observed: Windows Defender disabled before LSASS dump (IR-004). Tamper Protection and real-time monitoring disabled.
Observed: Defender disabled via Set-MpPreference and GUI before LSASS dump. Not primary detection target but observed during IR-004.
Observed: \WindowsUpdate task name masquerades as legitimate Windows Update process (IR-007).
Observed: Scheduled task named \WindowsUpdate to evade casual inspection (IR-007).
